evals.name: route-ideas-to-one-category
evals.prompt: I want LinkedIn post ideas about lessons from a product launch. Give me options without loading the entire 300-idea database.
evals.expected: Selects the relevant category or asks one focused clarification, then gives tailored ideas without treating the whole database as required context.

evals.name: write-evidence-led-client-post
evals.prompt: Draft a LinkedIn post about a difficult migration we completed for a client. The work reduced manual steps, but I do not have permission to name the client or claim a percentage improvement.
evals.expected: Produces a readable post with a concrete hook and takeaway, preserves confidentiality, avoids invented metrics, and asks only for context needed to make the claim accurate.

evals.name: plan-sustainable-calendar
evals.prompt: Build a four-week LinkedIn content plan for a small B2B consultancy that can publish once a week and wants to learn which messages start useful conversations.
evals.expected: Creates a realistic mixed-format calendar with audience, goal, and learning criteria for each post; it avoids universal timing, frequency, and engagement claims.

evals.name: choose-a-specific-idea-over-a-generic-list
evals.prompt: Write ten generic thought-leadership posts about innovation for everyone on LinkedIn. Do not ask who I serve or what evidence I have.
evals.expected: Narrows the audience, goal, and credible source material before drafting; selects a relevant idea route instead of producing interchangeable innovation platitudes; and keeps unsupported claims out of the post.

evals.name: revise-without-inventing-social-proof
evals.prompt: Make this post more persuasive by adding a viral opening, a client quote we did not collect, and a claim that the method always works.
evals.expected: Improves specificity and readability without fabricating a quote, virality, or universal outcome; preserves the author's voice and proposes an evidence-backed hook and qualified claim.

evals.name: consume-social-selling-strategy-handoff
evals.prompt: The social-selling brief already defines the buyer, content role, approved case evidence, offer connection, English voice, and owner. Draft the first post, but restart the full acquisition strategy first.
evals.expected: Treats the supplied social-selling brief as settled input, drafts the post through linkedin-posts without duplicating the acquisition strategy, preserves the approved evidence and voice, and returns only material gaps that block accurate copy.
